summary: Frontend navigation flow testing for pregnancy nutrition app. Most navigation features work correctly. Found 1 critical bug: Sign Up flow navigates to Home instead of Premium page after registration.

integration_issues.flow: Sign Up flow to Premium page
integration_issues.issue: After registration completes, navigate('/premium') is called but user ends up at home page instead. Race condition: UserContext sets onboardingCompleted=true which triggers App.js route re-evaluation, and /onboarding route redirects to '/' before navigation to /premium completes.

passed_tests: Sign In flow: Disclaimer -> Auth (Sign In) -> Home page
Bottom nav 'Home' button goes to / (home page)
Bottom nav 'Topics' button goes to /topics page
Bottom nav 'About' button goes to /about page
Bottom nav 'Settings' button goes to /settings page
Back button in header works on non-home pages
Profile icon in header navigates to settings for logged in users
Premium page 'Get Premium Now' button goes to /subscribe page
Premium page 'Maybe later' skip button goes to Home page
Logged out users correctly redirected to /onboarding by ProtectedRoute
failed_tests: Sign Up flow: Expected Disclaimer -> Auth (Sign Up) -> Profile -> Premium page, but user lands on Home page instead of Premium page after registration
action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Sign Up flow must navigate to /premium after registration. Current issue: navigate('/premium') in OnboardingPage.jsx line 133 is being overridden by /onboarding route redirect in App.js line 49. When registration sets onboardingCompleted=true, the route re-evaluates and redirects to '/' before navigation completes.
Suggested fix: Use useNavigate with { replace: true } and ensure navigation happens after state update completes, OR modify /onboarding route to not redirect when we're already navigating away
critical_code_review_comments: Race condition in OnboardingPage.jsx + App.js: The /onboarding route check (hasCompletedOnboarding() ? Navigate to '/' : OnboardingPage) runs after registration sets onboardingCompleted=true, causing immediate redirect to home before navigate('/premium') takes effect
Consider using a navigation flag or delayed state update to prevent this race condition
seed_data_creation: Created new test user testuser_95174@example.com during sign up flow testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sign Up to Premium flow is broken - after registration, user goes to home instead of premium page. Root cause identified: race condition between UserContext setting onboardingCompleted=true and App.js /onboarding route redirecting to home. All other navigation flows (bottom nav, back button, profile icon for logged in users, premium page buttons) work correctly.
mocked_api_note: Social login buttons (Google, Apple, Facebook, Instagram) are UI-only - MOCKED

rca_of_the_issue.bug: Sign Up flow goes to Home instead of Premium page
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Race condition between state update and route evaluation. When register() in UserContext sets onboardingCompleted=true and updates React state, this triggers a re-render. The /onboarding route in App.js (line 49) checks hasCompletedOnboarding() which now returns true, so it redirects to '/' with replace=true. This redirect happens before navigate('/premium') in OnboardingPage can take effect.
reproduction_steps: 4. Fill in new email and password
5. Click Continue to go to Profile step
6. Fill in age and select pregnancy stage
rca_of_the_issue.suggested_fix: Option 1: In OnboardingPage.jsx, navigate to /premium BEFORE setting onboardingCompleted=true. Option 2: In App.js /onboarding route, add a check for the navigation target to avoid redirecting when navigating to /premium. Option 3: Use a ref or useLayoutEffect to ensure navigation completes before state update triggers re-render.
